zh.Bus-lès-Artois, Somme, Hauts-de-France,: 法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆布-利斯-亞托斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Marie Du Bus於1096年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆布-利斯-亞托斯時，她的父親Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS42歲，母親Jehanne De Sarton25歲。她和Hector De Marieux至少有1個兒子。她去世於1152年在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆馬利額享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marie Du Bus于1096年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆布-利斯-亞托斯时，她的父亲Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS42岁，母亲Jehanne De Sarton25岁。她和Hector De Marieux至少有1个儿子。她去世于1152年在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆馬利額享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Bus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Jacobus, Sievert, Willem.  French Andre.
Dutch: variant of   Bos  .
Dutch: metonymic occupational name from bussemaker, maker of boxes and tins or of firearms, from (Middle) Dutch bus ‘box, tin, firearm’. See Buss .

variants.name: Buis
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from the ancient Germanic personal name Buso, see Bus .
Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a fisherman on a type of ship called a buis. Compare Bice , Bise , and Byce .

French: from buis ‘box (tree)’, in any of a number of possible applications. It may have been a topographic name for someone who lived by a box thicket, or a metonymic occupational name for a worker in the wood.

variants.name: Bos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Hendrik, Willem, Berend, Cor, Corniel, Derk, Geert, Gysbert, Klaas, Maarten, Piet.

Dutch, South German, and French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Boso or from a shortened form of Boshard, composed of the elements bōs ‘bad, evil, wild, angry’ (German böse, Dutch boos) and hard ‘hardy, brave’. Compare Boos .
Dutch: variant of   Bosch  .

variants.name: Bushek
meanings: Americanized form of Czech Bušek: from a pet form of the old personal name Buš (see Bus ).
Americanized form of Polish Buszek: from a pet form of the compound personal names like Budzisław (see Busko 1).
Americanized form of Buschek, a Germanized Czech, Polish, or other Slavic surname (see above).

nameMeanings.name: Marie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Maria . When first introduced to England in the Middle Ages, it was Anglicized in pronunciation and respelled Mary . This French form was reintroduced into the English-speaking world as a separate name in the 19th century, and is still pronounced more or less in the French manner, although sometimes with the stress on the first syllable. It is now often used in combination with other names such as Ellie, Chloe, and Lisa.

en.lifeSummary: When Marie Du Bus was born in 1096, in Bus-lès-Artois, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS, was 42 and her mother, Jehanne De Sarton, was 25. She had at least 1 son with Hector De Marieux. She died in 1152, in Marieux, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marie Du Bus est née en 1096, à Bus-lès-Artois, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Jehanne De Sarton, avait 25 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Hector De Marieux. Elle est décédée en 1152, à Marieux, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marie Du Bus 1096 in Bus-lès-Artois, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS 42 und ihre Mutter Jehanne De Sarton 25. Sie hatte mit Hector De Marieux mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1152 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Marieux, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marie Du Bus è nata il nel 1096 nel Bus-lès-Artois, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Jehanne De Sarton, aveva 25 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Hector De Marieux. Morì nel 1152, nel Marieux, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.Marieux, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France: フランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメマリーウックス

ja.lifeSummary: Marie Du Busが1096年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメブス-ルeス-アルトイスで生まれた時，父Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUSは42歳，母Jehanne De Sartonは25歳であった。彼女はHector De Marieuxとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1152年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメマリーウックスで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Marie Du Bus(이)가 1096년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 버스-e스-아르토이스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jehanne De Sarton(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 Hector De Marieux(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1152년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 마이우스에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marie Du Bus nasceu em 1096, em Bus-lès-Artois, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Jehanne De Sarton, tinha 25 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Hector De Marieux. Ela faleceu em 1152, em Marieux, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marie Du Bus родилась в 1096 г. в местности бус-лeс-артоис, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Jehanne De Sarton, 25. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Hector De Marieux. Она умерла в 1152 г. в местности мариеукс, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marie Du Bus nació en 1096, en Bus-lès-Artois, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Hugues ou Huon Ecuyer Sieur de Bus BUS, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Jehanne De Sarton, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Hector De Marieux. Murió en 1152, en Marieux, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 56 años.
